title: ranunculus falcatus subsp. incurvus
intro: Ranunculus falcatus subsp. incurvus, commonly known as Hooked Buttercup or Incurved Buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are thought to be due to the presence of compounds such as ranunculin and furocoumarins. The health benefits of Ranunculus falcatus subsp. incurvus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its possible antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of this plant have been used in the past in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use is not widely practiced today due to concerns about its toxicity and potential side effects.
